portwatch_chokepoints.note: Daily AIS-derived tanker counts. Anomaly = 7-day mean below threshold vs pre-crisis baseline OR 30d avg (ports avg>=5). T6-S45: 7d mean (was single-day, smooths ~5d feed lag) + strategic ports alert at -40% (0.60), others -50% (0.50).

items.title: Oil Market Loses Its Safety Net as Iran Conflict Reignites
items.summary: The global oil market is much more exposed to the latest re-escalation in the Middle East, as most of the buffers that cushioned the initial shock of the Iran war are wearing thin and unable to prevent the next oil price spike. For weeks, market p...

items.title: Why the EU Still Can’t Agree on Its Next Round of Russia Sanctions
items.summary: Many European Union diplomats thought that it would become easier to approve sanctions on Russia after the departure of former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban in May, after 16 years in power. Seen as the most Kremlin-friendly leader in the b...

items.title: New Zealand Oil Explorers Rush to Secure Offshore Permits Before Election
items.summary: Small oil and gas companies are vying to secure exploration permits offshore New Zealand in the coming months before a potential reinstatement of the drilling ban in the country. Last year, New Zealand reopened for oil and gas exploration, ready t...

items.title: Nigeria Targets Record 3 Million Bpd Oil Output by 2030
items.summary: Nigeria is set to ramp up crude oil production to 3 million barrels per day by 2030, according to the country’s upstream regulator, which says reforms aimed at accelerating investment and reducing project delays are beginning to reverse years of d...

items.title: Houthis Declare Naval Blockade on Saudi Arabia as Red Sea Crisis Deepens
items.summary: Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis declared an immediate naval blockade on Saudi Arabia on Monday, Reuters reported, bringing the U.S.-Iran war officially to the southern entrance of the Red Sea and threatening an export route used by Riyadh to bypass d...

items.title: New UK Prime Minister Set to Back North Sea Oil and Gas Projects
items.summary: Incoming prime minister Andy Burnham is set to announce new drilling for oil and gas in the North Sea within days of taking office as part of a policy blitz, according to reports. Burnham’s team has asked the civil service to draw up plans to appr...

items.title: Drone Strike Halts Exports at Strategic Black Sea Oil Terminal
items.summary: A drone struck a tanker loading crude oil at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s (CPC) Black Sea export terminal on Monday, according to regional media reports, forcing the suspension of exports for the second time in less than 24 hours. The CPC sai...

items.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Enters Dangerous New Phase With Fresh Missile Exchanges
items.summary: Air raid sirens blared in Bahrain and other Gulf states as the struggle for control of the Strait of Hormuz continued after US forces conducted a ninth day of air strikes on targets around Iran. Tensions increased over the weekend in the Gulf afte...

items.title: U.S. Gasoline Prices Climb Back Above $4 Per Gallon
items.summary: The national average price of gasoline in the United States topped $4 per gallon once again on Monday following the crude oil price rally of the past week amid the renewed hostilities in the Middle East and the fresh de facto closure of the Strait...

items.title: Brent Briefly Breaks $90 After Fresh Iran War Escalation
items.summary: Brent Crude prices briefly hit $90 per barrel in Asian trade on Monday as the Middle East conflict continued to escalate with fresh U.S. strikes on Iran and Tehran targeting American bases in the region and vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Both Br...

items.title: Europe Faces Diesel Crunch as Inventories Head Toward Multi-Year Lows
items.summary: European diesel markets are set to further tighten as multiple supply disruptions have combined to drag inventories to multi-year lows, analysts at Morgan Stanley say. “The picture is genuinely tight,” Morgan Stanley’s wrote in a note on Sunday ca...

items.title: India’s Crude Oil Import Bill Soars 60% as Iran War Drives Prices Higher
items.summary: India paid 60% more for crude oil imports in the April-June quarter compared to the same period last year, as the surge in oil prices couldn’t offset slightly lower import volumes. The value of India’s crude oil imports jumped in the first quarter...

defesa.label: Defesa

themes.note: Decomposicao em 8 temas da enciclopedia (T6-S26). Score=None quando dados insuficientes. Anti-synthetic: nao fabrica scores. Cada tema lista gaps_to_close_priority com APIs livres a integrar em S27+.
